question_text: A household uses two different heating systems. System A uses a 3 kW electric heater running for 4 hours per day. System B uses a 2 kW electric heater running for 6 hours per day. Over a 7-day week, which system uses more electrical energy, and by how much? Express your answer in kilowatt-hours (kWh).

options.label: A
options.text: System A uses 28 kWh more than System B

options.label: B
options.text: System B uses 28 kWh more than System A

options.label: C
options.text: System A uses 84 kWh and System B uses 84 kWh, so they use the same energy

options.label: D
options.text: System B uses 56 kWh more than System A over the week

correct_answer: C) System A uses 84 kWh and System B uses 84 kWh, so they use the same energy
explanation: System A: 3 kW × 4 hours × 7 days = 84 kWh per week. System B: 2 kW × 6 hours × 7 days = 84 kWh per week. Both systems use identical amounts of electrical energy even though they have different power ratings and running times. This demonstrates that different combinations of power and time can produce the same total energy consumption.
